metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the contradiction between the Scope section ('Failure paths are separate stories') and the TOCTOU AC (which specifies N=5 retry + park-on-exhaustion): either move the re-drive/park logic to a separate story and simplify the AC to 'do NOT submit, abort with needs_rebase', or explicitly expand the Scope to include 'TOCTOU re-drive loop and exhaustion handling'.
findings.finding: The 'Scope' section explicitly declares 'Failure paths... are separate stories,' but the TOCTOU guard AC specifies a detailed failure/retry path (N=5 re-drives, park on exhaustion, surface `needs_rebase`) that is a non-trivial failure handling routine. This contradiction sends the implementer in two directions: a scope-first reading excludes the retry/park logic entirely, while an AC-first reading requires implementing it.
findings.location: Scope section vs. AC: 'FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Option A (simpler story): Change the TOCTOU AC to 'if main advanced or SHA changed, do NOT submit — abort the current wipChain with status needs_rebase' and move the N=5 re-drive loop to a follow-on story. Option B (include it): Add 'TOCTOU re-drive loop (N=5)' to the Scope's happy-path list and rename it from purely 'happy-path' to 'core loop including TOCTOU re-drive'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Dependencies section: S1's `Autosubmit` vote ACL is granted to `Contributors` (requesters), not restricted to `autolander-bot`; update the description to 'the `Autosubmit` label + Contributors vote ACL' and verify no S2 AC silently relies on a bot-only vote ACL invariant that S1 does not deliver.
findings.finding: The plan's Dependencies section characterizes S1's output as an 'autolander-bot vote ACL', but S1's ticket (843b-801f-9ef9-442d) defines the Autosubmit vote ACL as granted to 'Contributors' (requesters, not bot-only): `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors`. This is an empirically-checkable assertion about a named dependency's output that the ticket probe directly refutes — the ACL is not bot-restricted.
findings.location: Dependencies section: 'the `Autosubmit` label + `autolander-bot` vote ACL'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'the `Autosubmit` label + `autolander-bot` vote ACL' to 'the `Autosubmit` label (contributor-votable, `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors`) + the bot's ability to read that label'; remove any implicit assumption that only the bot can set Autosubmit+1.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand `tested_sha` to `tested_shas: {change_id → sha}` (one entry per chain member) and update the TOCTOU guard to re-verify that every chain member's current revision still matches its entry before issuing POST /submit.
findings.finding: The singular `tested_sha` field in the wipChain schema cannot represent all post-rebase patchset SHAs for a multi-member relation chain, leaving N-1 chain members' SHA integrity unchecked in the TOCTOU guard before submit.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — wipChain schema AC and TOCTOU guard AC
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the scalar `tested_sha` with a map `tested_shas: dict[change_id, sha]` populated for all `chain_member_ids` after `/rebase:chain` completes. The TOCTOU pre-submit check must assert `currentRevision == tested_shas[id]` for every member; if any member drifted, trigger the re-drive loop.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rule in AC2 for hybrid chains (non-merge ancestors beneath a merge-tip change): specify whether (a) the service treats the presence of any merge member as grounds to reject/park the whole chain, (b) the non-merge sub-chain is rebased with /rebase:chain first then the merge tip is rebased individually, or (c) the entire chain is ineligible for /rebase:chain and each member is rebased individually.
findings.finding: The rebase-selection rule in AC2 has no defined branch for a hybrid relation chain — non-merge ancestor changes beneath a `--no-ff` merge change at the chain tip — leaving an implementer with contradictory instructions.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule AC (AC2)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the N=5 re-drive counter scope in the TOCTOU AC: state explicitly that N is per continuous wipChain tenure for a given change_id (resets when a new change is selected as wipChain, not on service restart), and that parking persists until the Autosubmit vote is cleared and re-cast.
findings.finding: The N=5 re-drive counter scope is unspecified — the plan does not state whether N resets per selection cycle (per change processed) or persists across selections — creating an ambiguous implementation that could either starve a legitimately busy change or permanently park it after service bounces.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive AC

findings.finding: 'Park the change and surface needs_rebase' is not a specific observable outcome — 'park' and 'surface' are effort-framed verbs with no defined system-state change, making the criterion non-measurable in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — FFO TOCTOU guard AC (N=5 exhaustion clause)

findings.finding: The Scope section explicitly excludes failure paths ('Failure paths, deploy, and the agent-facing command are separate stories'), yet the TOCTOU AC specifies a detailed failure-handling path: N=5 re-drives, a parking step, and surfacing 'needs_rebase'. An implementer reading Scope would exclude this work; an implementer reading the AC would include it — a direct contradiction between sections.
findings.location: Scope section vs. AC bullet 4 (FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'TOCTOU re-drive loop (up to N=5 retries) and parking on exhaustion are in scope as part of the happy-path guard.' Remove or adjust the statement that failure paths are separate, or clarify that only un-recoverable failures (e.g., network errors, Gerrit API failures) are deferred.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'Verification' section that provides at least one concrete proving command per AC (e.g. a `pytest tests/test_autolander_loop.py -k fifo_ordering` invocation with a stub Gerrit client, plus an end-to-end smoke command against a test Gerrit instance for the submit path).
findings.finding: No proving command exists for any acceptance criterion — the plan contains zero test strategy, CI check, or verification command. An agent completing this work has no deterministic way to produce evidence that each AC is satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all bullets)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a testing plan covering: (a) happy-path loop unit test (select→rebase→verified→submit), (b) failure paths (N=5 exhaustion, TOCTOU re-drive, carried-vote rejection, merge-change routing), and (c) at minimum a simulated-Gerrit integration test for the fresh-Verified detection to avoid a proxy-validation gap where mocks bypass the actual vote-timestamp comparison logic.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a complex state machine (5 phases, FIFO selection, merge-vs-chain routing, N=5 re-drive loop, TOCTOU guard, fresh-Verified detection) but contains zero mention of a testing approach — no unit tests, integration tests, or acceptance test strategy for any of the specified behaviors, including multiple explicit failure paths (N=5 exhaustion → park, TOCTOU guard → re-drive, merge change never using /rebase:chain).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all items; Scope section

findings.finding: No proving command exists for any of the seven acceptance criteria — there is no test script, integration harness, or CLI invocation described that would produce concrete evidence of correctness for any claim.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all seven ACs

findings.finding: The plan specifies no testing approach for any of the new logic: the wipChain state machine, FIFO selection, rebase-routing branching (merge vs chain vs single), fresh-Verified SHA detection, TOCTOU guard, or the N=5 re-drive failure path that parks the change and surfaces `needs_rebase`. The N=5 exhaustion behavior is a new user-facing failure flow with zero test coverage mentioned.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all ACs; Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'Unit tests cover rebase-routing (merge/chain/single), fresh-SHA Verified detection (carried-vote rejection), and wipChain phase transitions. An integration test drives the N=5 re-drive exhaustion path end-to-end against a Gerrit test double and asserts the change is parked with `needs_rebase`.'

findings.finding: The plan contains zero proving commands or described tests for any of its seven acceptance criteria — no executor can verify done-ness without inventing the entire test strategy.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all bullets)

findings.finding: No acceptance criterion has an associated proving command or verification step — the plan defines six behavioral ACs but provides zero concrete commands (unit test invocation, integration test, API query, log assertion) that an executor or reviewer can run to confirm each criterion is met.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all six ACs)

findings.finding: No testing plan exists for the substantial new state-machine logic introduced (wipChain phases, rebase-selection branching, fresh-Verified detection, TOCTOU guard, bounded re-drive loop). The plan contains zero mention of tests, test strategy, or test ACs, leaving the entire new behavior unvalidated.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all ACs) and Scope section
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Testing' section to the plan with: (1) unit tests for the three-way rebase-selection rule using mocked RelatedChanges responses; (2) unit tests for fresh-Verified detection (carried-vote vs. new-patchset-vote); (3) state-machine tests cycling through all phase transitions; (4) a failure-path test for re-drive bound exhaustion (expect idle/error phase); (5) a TOCTOU test where main advances between submit-check and submit, verifying re-drive is triggered rather than stale submit.

findings.finding: The plan introduces substantial new branching logic (rebase-selection rule, Verified-freshness check, TOCTOU guard, FIFO ordering) but contains zero testing plan — no unit tests, integration tests, or deferred test child ticket — leaving all failure paths (SHA mismatch, stale vote, main advancing mid-flight, chain vs. single-change detection) completely unspecified for coverage.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — all ACs; Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a maximum wait timeout for the 'awaiting_verified' phase (e.g., 2 hours), after which the change is parked with a 'ci_timeout' reason and the service transitions to 'idle' to serve the next change.
findings.finding: No timeout is defined for the 'awaiting_verified' phase. If CI is hung, the Verified vote never arrives and the service waits indefinitely, blocking the single serialized slot and preventing any other change from being processed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Awaits a fresh Verified +1' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'If no fresh Verified +1 arrives within T_verified_timeout (default 2h, configurable), park the wipChain change with label comment ci_timeout and reset phase to idle.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact Gerrit API mechanism for FIFO ordering: name the query option (e.g., `o=DETAILED_LABELS`), the response field carrying the per-label vote timestamp (e.g., `labels.Autosubmit.all[].date`), and whether sorting is client-side after the query — so the AC is implementable and verifiable.
findings.finding: The FIFO ordering requirement — 'keyed on the Autosubmit vote's timestamp (oldest-voted-submittable-first)' — is an unverified capability assertion about the Gerrit query API. The standard GET /changes/?q=label:Autosubmit+1+is:submittable response does not include per-label vote timestamps in its default output; the DETAILED_LABELS option is required to expose them, and even then client-side sorting is needed. The plan does not specify which API option, response field, or sort strategy delivers this FIFO ordering, leaving the implementation undefined and the capability unconfirmed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — first bullet: 'FIFO ordering keyed on the Autosubmit vote's timestamp (oldest-voted-submittable-first)'
findings.suggested_fix: Amend the FIFO AC to read: 'FIFO ordering keyed on the Autosubmit vote timestamp — retrieved via GET /changes/?q=label:Autosubmit+1+is:submittable&o=DETAILED_LABELS, using the `labels.Autosubmit.all[].date` field of the +1 vote entry; sorted client-side ascending (oldest first).'

findings.finding: The plan introduces new live Gerrit API boundaries (POST /rebase, POST /rebase:chain, POST /submit, Verified vote polling) but specifies no testing strategy at all — not even a thin acceptance test. With no stated live/end-to-end criterion, the story can be closed green with offline-mock-only tests that never exercise the actual Gerrit submit path, satisfying all ACs while leaving the live integration path completely untested.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all ACs) / Scope section — testing approach absent throughout
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'A smoke/integration test against a test Gerrit instance (or replay fixture capturing real Gerrit HTTP responses) executes the full select→rebase→await-Verified→submit loop and asserts the submitted SHA matches the rebased patchset SHA.' If live testing is deferred, name the child ticket explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `parked` (or `needs_rebase`) terminal phase value to the `phase` enum in AC5 to match the parking outcome specified in AC4.
findings.finding: The TOCTOU guard (AC4) specifies a 'park the change and surface `needs_rebase`' terminal outcome after N=5 re-drives, but the `phase` enum defined in AC5 contains no `parked` or `needs_rebase` phase value — the schema literally cannot represent this terminal failure state.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 ('park the change and surface needs_rebase') and AC5 (phase enum: {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, idle})
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC5: `phase ∈ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, parked, idle}` and add a note that `parked` is the terminal failure state entered after `rebase_attempts` reaches N=5, with `wipChain.tested_sha` cleared and the change left for operator action.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State a default maximum wait time for the `awaiting_verified` phase (e.g. 'wait up to 2 hours; if no fresh Verified arrives, park the wipChain — handled per failure-path story') so the executor has a concrete default to code against.
findings.finding: The 'awaiting_verified' phase has no timeout or maximum-wait bound stated for the happy path, meaning the loop can wait indefinitely for a Verified vote that never arrives even within the in-scope scenario.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Awaits a fresh Verified +1' bullet; wipChain schema bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the exact Gerrit query string and option flags (e.g., `?q=label:Autosubmit+1+is:submittable&o=DETAILED_LABELS&S=0`) that expose per-account vote timestamps for FIFO ordering, confirmed against the Gerrit 3.14.1 REST API reference, and add this as an empirical precondition.
findings.finding: The plan asserts that the FIFO change-selection query uses `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` as a Gerrit query string, and that ordering by the `Autosubmit` vote's timestamp is available. The plan does not state which query parameter or sort field achieves FIFO-by-vote-timestamp (e.g., `o=DETAILED_LABELS` to expose per-account timestamps, or a specific sort order). This is an unverified query surface on an unreleased label — the `Autosubmit` label does not yet exist on the server (depends on S1), so the query cannot be tested until S1 lands. Auth preconditions for this authenticated query are also not stated.
findings.location: AC 1: 'Selects the front label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable change/chain; FIFO ordering keyed on the Autosubmit vote's timestamp (oldest-voted-submittable-first)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a crash-recovery strategy for the in-memory wipChain: either persist phase transitions to durable storage (file/DB) so restarts can resume, or specify a safe startup scan that parks any change found in a non-idle phase and surfaces a 'service_restarted' signal.
findings.finding: The wipChain is held only in memory with no durability or crash-recovery mechanism. If the service process crashes or restarts during any phase (rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting), all in-flight state is lost and the change is stranded — neither submitted nor parked — with no indication to the user.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — wipChain schema bullet: 'a single in-memory record … held by the single instance'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that after N=5 re-drives the wipChain is reset to idle (all fields cleared) immediately after the park/needs_rebase action, so the loop can select the next eligible change.
findings.finding: The wipChain retirement path after the N=5 re-drive limit is unspecified: the plan says 'park the change and surface needs_rebase' but does not say whether wipChain is reset to idle afterward, leaving it potentially stuck in a non-idle phase and blocking all future selections indefinitely.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the re-drive bullet: 'After parking, reset wipChain to the idle sentinel so the loop proceeds to select the next front Autosubmit+1 change.'

findings.finding: The `awaiting_verified` phase in the `wipChain` state machine has no defined timeout or escape path: if CI is permanently broken or never posts a `Verified +1` on the rebased patchset, `wipChain` stays in `awaiting_verified` indefinitely. The state machine's exit transitions for this phase are incomplete.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC5 (wipChain schema / phase definition) and AC3 (awaiting fresh Verified)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that exercises the rebase → await-Verified → submit path end-to-end against a staging or sandboxed Gerrit instance (or document an explicit rationale for deferring this to a named child story).
findings.finding: No end-to-end exercise criterion covers the live Gerrit API path (rebase → await Verified → submit). Offline/mocked tests can pass while the actual Gerrit integration is broken.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — rebase, awaiting_verified, and submit bullets

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the intended invocation model (e.g. 'polling every 30 s' or 'Gerrit stream-events webhook') and a maximum polling interval as a default the executor can act on.
findings.finding: The invocation mechanism (polling vs webhook, polling interval) is unspecified, leaving the executor to make a load-bearing design choice with no stated default.
findings.location: What / Scope sections
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'Polling loop with a configurable interval (default: 30 s); reads `gerrit stream-events` or falls back to REST polling — decision: REST polling for Phase 1 simplicity.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Probe `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related` on the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance for a known chain, capture the actual response JSON schema, and document the specific fields the autolander will use for chain detection; add this as an empirical precondition to the plan.
findings.finding: The plan uses `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related` (the RelatedChanges endpoint) to detect relation chains, but this is a new endpoint surface never used by the existing `gerrit_client.py`. The plan does not state which fields in the response body it will inspect to detect merge commits vs. non-merge members, nor whether the response shape on Gerrit 3.14.1 matches its expected structure. Evidence class: Unverified (endpoint recalled from Gerrit REST docs, not observed from the live instance).
findings.location: AC: 'Rebase-selection rule' bullet — 'detect a relation chain via GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related (the RelatedChanges endpoint)'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `rebase_attempts` (or equivalent) counter field to the `wipChain` schema in AC5, so the N=5 re-drive bound in AC4 has a clearly named, co-located storage slot.
findings.finding: The `wipChain` schema (AC5) has no field for the re-drive attempt counter, yet the TOCTOU guard protocol (AC4) requires bounding the loop to 'at most N=5 times' — an implementer cannot determine whether this counter is ephemeral local state, a named field in `wipChain`, or something else.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 (TOCTOU guard / re-drive loop) and AC5 (wipChain schema)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the AC5 schema to: `{change_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_sha, verified_at, phase, rebase_attempts}` and add a note that `rebase_attempts` is incremented on each `rebasing` phase entry and reset to 0 on `idle`.

findings.finding: The re-drive counter `N=5` is referenced as a loop bound in AC4's TOCTOU re-drive protocol, but the `wipChain` schema defined in AC5 (`{change_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_sha, verified_at, phase}`) contains no counter field. An implementer must invent a name and placement for this counter — it is structurally absent from the schema that owns the state machine.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 (FFO TOCTOU guard) and AC5 (wipChain schema)
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the AC5 schema to: `{change_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_sha, verified_at, phase, redriveCount}` where `redriveCount` is an integer initialized to 0 at `selecting`, incremented by 1 each time AC4 re-drives (resets `phase` to `rebasing`), and the guard fires when `redriveCount >= N`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the full CREATE leg: specify that wipChain is initialized to `{phase: idle, change_id: null, …}` at service startup and re-initialized to the same idle sentinel immediately after a successful submit or a failed N=5 park.
findings.finding: The wipChain record's creation/initialization lifecycle is unspecified: the plan does not say when the record is first created (service start? first select call?), what its initial state is, or how it is re-initialized after a successful submit — leaving the CREATE leg of the shared-state lifecycle open.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — wipChain schema bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit note: 'On service start, wipChain is initialized to `{phase: idle, change_id: None, chain_member_ids: [], tested_sha: None, verified_at: None}`; after submit succeeds or after N=5 park, reset all fields to this idle sentinel before the next select iteration.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either derive N=5 from a spike/measurement of observed main-branch advancement rates during rebase windows, or expose it as a named configuration value (e.g., `MAX_REDRIVE_ATTEMPTS`) so it can be tuned without a code change.
findings.finding: The re-drive limit `N=5` is asserted as a hardcoded constant in the AC with no measurement rationale and no specification that it be configurable — yet it is a threshold that will likely need tuning once real rebase-contention patterns are observed.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive AC: 'looping at most N=5 times'
findings.suggested_fix: Expose `MAX_REDRIVE_ATTEMPTS` as a service config parameter (env var or config file key) with a default of 5; add a TODO/spike note to measure actual contention frequency in the first week of production to validate or adjust the default.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit retry/backoff (e.g., exponential with jitter, max 3 attempts) for every Gerrit REST call, and define what phase the wipChain transitions to on exhausted retries.
findings.finding: No retry/backoff/circuit-breaker is specified for any Gerrit REST API call (related-changes, rebase, verified-poll, submit). A transient 5xx or network error on any of these calls has no stated handling — the plan neither retries nor surfaces the error, leaving the service's behavior undefined on transient failures.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — rebase-selection rule, awaiting fresh Verified, ancestor-atomic submit AC bullets
findings.suggested_fix: For each API call category: (a) GET calls — retry up to 3× with exponential backoff, fail to 'idle' + alert on exhaustion; (b) POST /rebase — retry idempotently (rebase is safe to retry if patchset unchanged); (c) POST /submit — do NOT retry blindly; instead re-check submission status via GET before deciding, since the submit may have succeeded.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the restart-reconciliation policy: specify whether on startup the loop (a) treats any in-flight wipChain as lost and re-selects from scratch (safe only if Gerrit submit is idempotent/detectable), or (b) reads Gerrit state to reconstruct phase before resuming.
findings.finding: No crash-recovery or restart-resilience path is defined for the in-memory wipChain: if the service process dies while in the 'awaiting_verified' or 'rebasing' phase, the record is lost and the plan does not specify how the loop behaves on restart — risking either a stuck Gerrit change (never submitted, never parked) or a double-submit if Gerrit already accepted a prior POST /submit.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — wipChain schema bullet; Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `infra/gerrit/project.config` and `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` as edit targets and specify which Gerrit group the `autolander-bot` service account must join (or what new Submit ACL line is needed) so that `POST /submit` succeeds.
findings.finding: The plan's new auto-lander service will need to call `POST /submit` on Gerrit, which requires the Submit ACL. The current `infra/gerrit/project.config` grants Submit only to group `Contributors` + `Administrators`. An `autolander-bot` service account must be a member of one of these groups to submit — but neither S1 nor S2 names provisioning the autolander-bot into `Contributors` (or granting it Submit via a new ACL line). This is an unenumerated consumer of `infra/gerrit/project.config` and `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh`.
findings.location: AC: 'Ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA' / infra/gerrit/project.config lines 62-64
findings.suggested_fix: Add an explicit AC or 'Scope' note: 'The autolander-bot service account must be added to a group that holds the Submit ACL (e.g., `Contributors` or a new `autolander` group with an explicit `submit = group autolander` line in `project.config`); `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` and `project.config` are edit targets for this provisioning.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` capability assertion: cite the Gerrit REST API docs (version-pinned) confirming this parameter preserves commit-message trailers (Signed-off-by, rebar-ticket) for both `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` and `POST /rebase:chain`, OR add a spike/integration test that validates trailer preservation end-to-end before this AC is marked complete.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer' as a given, but cites no Gerrit documentation, version constraint, or spike verifying this behavior for both POST /changes/{id}/rebase and POST /rebase:chain. Prior review cache for parent ticket f1fa-57d8-07cb-44af already flagged the identical assertion as unresolved. If the parameter does not preserve commit-message trailers (e.g., Gerrit re-authors the rebase commit under the bot identity and strips the uploader's trailers), DCO compliance breaks silently.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-selection rule: 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a verify_command (e.g., a REST API call against the live Gerrit instance with on_behalf_of_uploader=true on a test change) that confirms the rebased patchset's commit message retains the original uploader's Signed-off-by and rebar-ticket trailer. Pin to the Gerrit version in use (3.14.x) since behavior has changed across releases.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the exact JSON body field name for `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` (specifically `on_behalf_of_uploader`) against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 REST API reference or a live probe before implementing the rebase step, and add this to the plan as an empirical precondition.
findings.finding: The plan's core rebase step uses `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with the `on_behalf_of_uploader=true` parameter, but this is a new endpoint surface never exercised by the existing `gerrit_client.py`. The specific parameter name `on_behalf_of_uploader` is UNVERIFIED against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 REST API docs or the running instance — no codebase search, doc probe, or smoke-check script references this field name. A prior review pass on parent epic `f1fa` (cache entry `add9ca3a`) already flagged this as unverified for trailer-preservation semantics, indicating this gap has persisted across revisions.
findings.location: AC: 'Rebase-selection rule' bullet — 'on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves author/DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a spike AC: using `curl -u bot:token -X POST .../changes/<test-id>/rebase -d '{"on_behalf_of_uploader": true}'` on the staging Gerrit, observe the response schema and resulting patchset commit trailers. Document the confirmed field name and behavior in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `POST /rebase:chain` (or its exact equivalent) is available and functional on the project's Gerrit 3.14.1 instance by running a live probe (e.g., `curl -X POST .../changes/<chain-tip-id>/rebase:chain`) and document the result as an empirical precondition in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan names `POST /rebase:chain` as the mechanism for rebasing a non-merge relation chain, but this endpoint (a Gerrit custom verb using the `:chain` suffix on the base `/rebase` resource) is UNVERIFIED — the existing codebase has no prior usage, no doc reference, and no smoke-check script probing it. The `:chain` action is specific to Gerrit's chain-rebase plugin or a server capability flag, and its availability on the project's Gerrit 3.14.1 instance is unconfirmed.
findings.location: AC: 'Rebase-selection rule' bullet — 'a chain of >1 non-merge member rebases with POST /rebase:chain'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan which Gerrit group the autolander-bot service account will be added to (or what new ACL line will be added to `infra/gerrit/project.config`) to grant it the Submit permission, and name `infra/gerrit/project.config` and `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` as edit targets for this story.
findings.finding: The plan's TOCTOU guard re-verifies `is:submittable` and then calls `POST /submit` (implied as `POST /changes/{id}/submit`), but this endpoint is not used anywhere in the existing codebase. More importantly, the plan does not state what auth identity performs the submit — the existing `project.config` restricts Submit exclusively to group `Contributors` + Administrators (lines 62–64: `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit`). The autolander-bot service account must be in one of these groups to submit, but the plan says nothing about provisioning the bot's Submit ACL, and neither S1 (which adds the `Autosubmit` vote ACL for `Contributors`) nor this plan names adding the autolander-bot to the Submit grant.
findings.location: AC: 'Ancestor-atomic submit of the exact tested SHA. FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive' / infra/gerrit/project.config lines 62-64
findings.suggested_fix: Add `submit = group Service Users` under `[access "refs/heads/*"]` in `project.config` (or add the autolander-bot specifically to `Contributors`), and reference this as a prerequisite AC alongside the S1 dependency.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'Credential Management' section specifying that the `autolander-bot` Gerrit token is injected via a secrets manager (e.g., Vault, GCP Secret Manager) at runtime, never stored in code or config files, and that the HTTP client is configured to suppress Authorization headers from debug logs.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a service that authenticates to Gerrit as `autolander-bot` (capable of submitting code) but says nothing about how that credential is stored, injected, or prevented from appearing in logs or config files.
findings.location: Dependencies / Scope sections — implicit bot credential use; no secrets-management section exists
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC or implementation note: "The `autolander-bot` HTTP credential MUST be sourced from the project's secrets manager at startup (not from a checked-in config file or environment literal). The HTTP client MUST NOT log `Authorization` headers at any log level."

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR (or named design-doc section) capturing the autolander service's state machine, rebase-dispatch rule (merge vs non-merge), FIFO selection contract, and re-drive semantics so these decisions are discoverable beyond this story ticket.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new project service with a novel state machine (`wipChain` phases), a rebase-dispatch rule (merge vs non-merge), FIFO selection contract, and ancestor-atomic re-drive semantics — but names no ADR, design doc, or CLAUDE.md update to capture these architectural decisions.
findings.location: ## What, ## Acceptance Criteria (wipChain schema AC, rebase-selection rule AC)
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/NNNN-autolander-core-loop.md` covering: (1) wipChain state machine and phase transitions, (2) merge-change rebase dispatch rationale (MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE, never rebase:chain), (3) FIFO ordering contract (Autosubmit vote timestamp), (4) re-drive loop design and park condition. Reference it from this plan and from CLAUDE.md if the project uses that.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability AC: every phase transition, every parked change (with reason), and every exhausted retry must emit a structured log event; at minimum, a counter metric for 'changes_parked_by_reason' and 'api_errors_by_endpoint' must be defined.
findings.finding: No observability (metrics, structured logs, traces, or alerts) is specified for any failure point or phase transition in the loop. Operators have no defined way to detect that the service is stuck, that a change was parked, or that API errors are occurring.
findings.location: Entire plan — no observability section or AC

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a runtime kill-switch (e.g., an env-var flag or a config file checked each loop iteration) that halts new change selection without stopping the process, enabling safe disablement without a redeploy.
findings.finding: No feature flag, enable/disable switch, or canary mechanism is specified for activating the auto-lander loop in production. The service, once deployed, begins processing all label:Autosubmit+1 changes immediately with no gradual rollout path.
findings.location: Scope section; no rollout section present
findings.suggested_fix: Add AC: 'The loop checks a AUTOLANDER_ENABLED flag (env var, default true) at the top of each select iteration; setting it to false causes the loop to sleep and not select new wipChains, while completing any in-flight wipChain safely.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one AC with a concrete validation mechanism — e.g., 'given two simultaneous Autosubmit+1 chains, the service serializes them (second waits behind wipChain), demonstrable via a two-session integration test or an ops log showing sequential rebase timestamps' — so the stated benefit (no more hand-rolling / racing) is directly checkable.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism appears in any AC — the ACs are all system-internal state assertions (wipChain fields, phase transitions, rebase routing rules) with no before/after workflow comparison, operational metric, or dogfooding criterion that would let an observer confirm the named beneficiary (parallel agent sessions) actually stops hand-rolling or racing.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all items)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'Verified end-to-end by an integration test that submits two Autosubmit+1 changes concurrently and asserts that (a) only one wipChain is active at any moment and (b) both changes land on main in FIFO order without manual intervention.' This directly validates the named beneficiary's problem.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the autolander service will import and extend the existing `GerritClient` (`src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py`) for its Gerrit REST calls, or define a separate client; if separate, document why reuse is not appropriate (e.g. packaging boundary).
findings.finding: The plan places the new autolander service 'project infra — NOT src/rebar core' but is silent on whether it will reuse or extend the existing production `GerritClient` (src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py) for Gerrit REST plumbing. That class already provides authenticated requests, XSSI stripping, `GerritError`, and the Basic-auth header pattern — all of which the autolander also needs for POST /rebase, GET /related, Verified-vote polling, and POST /submit. Rebuilding Gerrit HTTP plumbing from scratch in the new service would be NIH relative to a tested, 4-site production class.
findings.location: What section ('A project service (Python, project infra — NOT src/rebar core)') and AC rebase-selection rule / submit AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to the 'What' or 'Scope' section: 'The service extends `rebar.review_bot.gerrit_client.GerritClient` with the additional methods it needs (POST /rebase, GET /related, POST /submit, Verified label poll), rather than reimplementing Gerrit HTTP plumbing.' If a packaging boundary genuinely prevents import, document that constraint explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name at minimum one concrete file path for the new service (e.g., `infra/autolander/autolander.py` or equivalent) so that the edit target is unambiguous and downstream deploy/test stories can reference it.
findings.finding: The plan describes a new deployable Python service ('project infra') but names zero concrete file paths (no module path, no Dockerfile, no compose service entry). The existing deployment pattern requires at minimum a compose service stanza in `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` and a Dockerfile. The plan's 'deploy is a separate story' scope cut does not exempt the service from needing these artifacts to run at all — they are pre-conditions for any in-process test or integration. These consumers of the compose/infra layer are entirely unenumerated.
findings.location: What section: 'A project service (Python, project infra)' / Scope section: 'deploy ... [is a] separate story'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale (1–3 sentences in 'What' or a new 'Why this mechanism' subsection) explaining why in-memory single-instance state is the right fit here — e.g., crash-recovery is out of scope for Phase 1, the service re-selects from Gerrit's authoritative state on restart, and a queue is cost-disproportionate at this change rate.
findings.finding: The plan gives no positive rationale for choosing an in-memory, single-instance service design over alternatives (e.g., persistent-state tracking or integration into the existing review-bot container), leaving implementers without a defensible design basis.
findings.location: What / Scope sections
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the 'What' section: 'In-memory state is sufficient because on restart the service re-selects from Gerrit's authoritative `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` query; crash-recovery and persistence are deferred to a later story. A single instance is chosen over a queue because the project's change rate (see ADR-0040) does not justify queue complexity, and serialization eliminates rebase races at the cost of throughput — an explicit and accepted tradeoff (see ADR-0040 R4).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare that all Gerrit API calls MUST use HTTPS (TLS ≥ 1.2) and that the HTTP client rejects plaintext `http://` base URLs at startup (fail-fast config validation).
findings.finding: The plan specifies Gerrit REST API calls (`GET /changes/…`, `POST /rebase`, `POST /submit`) over a network boundary but does not declare that HTTPS (TLS) is required, leaving the transport protection posture undeclared.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — rebase-selection rule and ancestor-atomic submit AC lines
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the implementation notes or a new 'Transport' AC: "The Gerrit base URL MUST be `https://…`; the service MUST refuse to start if the configured URL uses a plaintext scheme. TLS certificate verification MUST NOT be disabled."

findings.finding: `needs_rebase` is referenced in AC4 as the terminal output signal after N=5 re-drives ('surface `needs_rebase`'), but its type, mechanism, and vocabulary are never defined anywhere in the plan. It is an opaque string that an implementer cannot concretely act on.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4 (FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive)

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
